question:
Will Auckland FC win on 2025-11-01?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 1, 2025
If Auckland FC wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

Resolution source: https://www.a-league.com.au/

evidences.title: Injuries put pressure on Auckland FC and Melbourne City for A ...
evidences.content: , News | Both teams are likely to depend on youngsters when Auckland FC host men’s A-League leaders Melbourne City at Go Media Stadium Mt Smart on Saturday. Injuries are stretching both squads, forcing rival coaches Steve Corica (Auckland FC) and Aurelio Vidmar (Melbourne City) to look to young players. Main photo: Auckland FC will host Melbourne City at Go Media Stadium Mt Smart on Saturday. Third-placed Auckland FC will be without captain Hiroki Sakai (concussion), defender Dan Hall (broken ankle) and midfielder Cam Howieson (calf injury), and defensive midfielder Adama Coulibaly (suspended). READ MORE: Captain Hiroki Sakai to miss Auckland FC’s big clash with Melbourne City >>>> READ MORE: Auckland FC debutant Adama Coulibaly cops suspension after sending off >>>> [...] In recent games, Melbourne have looked to youth to see them through, giving debuts to Lawrence Wong (17) and Kavian Rahmani (18), and giving regular starts to Medin Memeti (17) and Arion Sulemani (19) have impressed, while Mikey Ghossaini (17) and Ben Dunbar (18). #### Squads Please note: The following squad has been advised to the A-League by Auckland FC but may not be accurate (see above story). evidences.title: Expansion of the A-League Men — Grokipedia
evidences.content: from three A-League championships. In its inaugural 2024–25 season, Auckland FC demonstrated rapid competitiveness, finishing with a strong home record of 8 wins, 4 draws, and 1 loss, including a 6–1 victory over Wellington Phoenix on February 22, 2025.(#ref-57)](#ref-57) The team achieved New Zealand's first A-League silverware, underscoring the expansion's success in attracting talent and fan interest, with average attendances exceeding 18,000.(#ref-58)](#ref-58) This performance defied historical challenges for new entrants, supported by Foley's multi-club model leveraging resources from Bournemouth for scouting and development.(#ref-59)](#ref-59) [...] outcomes among newer clubs. While earlier additions like Western United and Macarthur FC have averaged below the league's core teams—contributing to a 2023–24 overall average of 8,076 when legacy clubs alone reached 9,232—the 2024 entry of Auckland FC markedly boosted figures, with home averages exceeding 18,000 and peaking as the league's highest at 19,534 early in the season.(#ref-116)](#ref-116)(#ref-59)](#ref-59)(#ref-117)](#ref-117) This New Zealand market entry defied historical challenges for expansion sides, drawing se...

evidences.title: Auckland FC to hold open day at men's National League match against Phoenix - Friends of Football
evidences.content: “Most of the first team should be in attendance to support the reserves, giving you plenty of opportunities to meet your heroes and get acquainted with the new signings,” the club says. “The mini-matchday will aim to replicate, albeit on a smaller scale, the fun and excitement fans can expect at Go Media Stadium when the first team play at home for the first time this season on Saturday October 25.” Auckland FC Reserves and the Phoenix Reserves both lost their opening games in the men’s National League, and they’ll be chasing their first points in the 10-round competition when they meet. Both sides have players on international duty, with the national team at the FIFA U-20 Men’s World Cup in Chile. [...] # Auckland FC to hold open day at men’s National League match against Phoenix A-League club Auckland FC will host a family-friendly open day ahead of their men’s National League match against the Wellington Phoenix. The club will provide two hours of activities at Fred Taylor Park, Whenuapai, before Auckland FC Reserves take on the Phoenix Reserves at 3pm, on Saturday October 4, 2025. evidences.title: A-League schedule released after delays | RNZ
evidences.content: The men's 2025/26 season starts on 17 October and is a 26-round regular season. Each club will play 13 home games and 13 away games. With Western United's late withdrawal from this season's competition each team will play seven teams twice and four clubs three times. Auckland FC begin their second season in the league away against the team that ultimately ended their last season, Melbourne Victory, on 18 October. On the same day the Wellington Phoenix are also away to Perth Glory. From round two the Phoenix play six of their next eight matches at home at Sky Stadium, including the first of three derby games against Auckland FC on 8 November. Auckland host the derby on 6 December and it returns to Wellington on 21 February. [...] Melbourne celebrate their goal. The A-League Men was established to replace the National Soccer League as the top division in the Australian football league system. It is the only professional football league in the country. Founded in 2004 with eight teams commencing competition in the 2005–06 season, the league has since expanded into new markets across Australia and New Zealand. evidences.title: Auckland City FC: 'We are the working-class team at the FIFA Club ...
evidences.content: This is the world from which City’s players come — and they are not to be confused with the other team from Auckland. Auckland FC are a franchise founded in 2024 that competes in the mostly Australian A-League. As a result, they are not eligible for the Oceania Champions League from which City qualified for this Club World Cup. That is not the only difference. Advertisement Auckland FC are a fully professional team owned by Bill Foley, the American billionaire who also owns Bournemouth in the Premier League and ice hockey’s Vegas Golden Knights in the NHL, plus stakes in French side Lorient and Hibernian in Scotland. They play in a 25,000-capacity stadium and are sponsored by blue-chip companies including McDonald’s and New Balance. [...] That will likely be a tender moment. Three years ago, Kilkolly was in the middle of a training session when he received a call to tell him his brother had taken his own life. Last year, his father passed away in April, having been diagnosed with cancer two months earlier. Through the shock and lasting grief, football has provided sanctuary. Advertisement “I come from a region where you have to be resilient, and you are taught early that life is...
